name: Arrow of time / low-entropy initial state
summary: The fundamental laws of physics (CPT, Lorentz invariance, GR + SM equations) are time-symmetric, yet the universe exhibits a strong thermodynamic arrow of time toward higher entropy. This requires the initial state of the universe to have been an exceptionally low-entropy configuration relative to the equilibrium it could have started in. Penrose estimates the prior probability of the observed Big Bang state at one part in 10^(10^123) compared to the maximum-entropy state of equivalent matter content — the most extreme fine-tuning in physics.

characteristic_numbers.note: Penrose (1989) estimate: BB initial state is exponentially-of-exponentially atypical relative to maximum-entropy equilibrium of the same total energy. The Weyl curvature was nearly zero at BB while diverging in BH singularities (where entropy is maximised).

principles_stressed.note: Most extreme fine-tuning known. Possible resolutions: (a) inflation provides a smoothing mechanism, but only solves the homogeneity / flatness problems, not the underlying entropy puzzle; (b) eternal inflation populates many initial conditions, with anthropic selection picking low-entropy starts; (c) Penrose's CCC posits that a previous-aeon late-time low-entropy state matches conformally to the next aeon's BB; (d) the arrow of time may be illusory or local.

history: Boltzmann (1896) recognised the conflict. Penrose's 'Weyl curvature hypothesis' (1979 'General Relativity: An Einstein Centenary Survey'; 1989 'The Emperor's New Mind') gave the quantitative estimate and tied the puzzle to gravitational entropy. Carroll-Chen (2004), 'Spontaneous inflation and the origin of the arrow of time', arXiv:hep-th/0410270, propose an eternal-inflation answer. Foundational issue without consensus.

references.citation: Penrose, 'Singularities and time-asymmetry', in 'General Relativity: An Einstein Centenary Survey' (eds. Hawking, Israel), Cambridge University Press (1979)
Carroll and Chen, 'Spontaneous inflation and the origin of the arrow of time' (2004)
